sx-a162502d94: actions tracked by default on paths
actions no longer tracked by default; add angr.options.refs to state to re-enable
sx-b7c96ab40f: erroredpath subclass of path placed in errored stash of path group
errorrecord object (not a simstate subclass) placed in simgr.errored list (not a stash)
sx-9061ce16fd: simprocedures organized strictly by library (e.g. simprocedures['libc.so.6'])
simprocedures organized by standard (e.g. sim_procedures['libc'], sim_procedures['posix'], sim_procedures['glibc'])
sx-7dea01ee3d: hook_symbol redoes relocations for the symbol
hook_symbol hooks directly over the symbol address without redoing relocations
sx-105604ddc6: whatever you were doing before to look up a symbol
sx-81f8c8ce42: plain string literals (unicode) in solver comparisons
sx-4be771c1fe: memory.load(addr, n) -> bytes (no change in signature, but context implies changes in underlying behavior)
sx-0d4c17c521: pack_word(addr, value) and unpack_word(addr) with no extra parameters
pack_word and unpack_word now take optional size, endness, and signed parameters
sx-e8128262f0: memory.backers() -> iter[(start, bytearray)] (no optional address parameter)
memory.backers() now accepts an optional address parameter to skip backers ending before that address
sx-49d69b33fc: base_addr, entry_point, offset, arch, ld_path (without custom_ prefix)
sx-5184233c7d: all functions deprecated at or before the angr 7 release
sx-23a5fadf54: simcc could be customized via sp_delta, args, or ret_val parameters
simcc can no longer be customized with sp_delta, args, or ret_val; use simccusercall instead
sx-d9acaedc38: pass type info to calling convention
sx-3cffc41faf: save_unconstrained flag now on by default
sx-49f1fbb53d: simulation manager silently ignored incorrect keyword arguments
simulation manager throws an error on incorrect keyword arguments
sx-9332e983e3: unsatisfiable states only appeared in 'unsat' stash when save_unsat was set
unsatisfiable states always appear in 'unsat' stash regardless of save_unsat setting
sx-860bcbdfb7: state.options was a set of strings
state.options is now a simstateoptions instance
sx-38b22acef9: angr bound to vex ir for lifting native code
sx-c2ec227558: simrun class with subclasses instantiated from an input state
sx-5b117fc866: old way of calling inline simprocedures
create a simprocedure, then call execute() on it with a program state and arguments
sx-a2a6011a42: state.regs.eax (always triggers siminspect breakpoints and creates actions)
state.regs._eax or state._ip (prefix with underscore to avoid breakpoints/actions)
sx-6d7ee34823: project.is_hooked(addr) to check for syscall simprocedure
sx-f2533ef0ef: labels are now stored in knowledgebase
sx-a9152bf2f6: allowed state as fallback source of instruction bytes when no cle backer available
makes instruction lifting use the state as the source of bytes always
sx-da0624dc0b: syscalls produced a simrun for a syscall helper
syscalls produce a simprocedure for the given syscall, each syscall has its own address in a syscalls segment, and syscalls are treated as jumps to this segment
sx-73fc6e015c: syscall simprocedure address was the address right after the syscall instruction
each syscall simprocedure has its own address
sx-14d31da3f4: __div__ now performs signed division; use sdiv for unsigned division
sx-b7932c3cac: old keyword parameter names for irsb
new keyword parameter names (data, mem_addr, arch)
sx-13f7861268: based on llvm 16 (arm, ppc) or llvm 18 (others)
sx-68cfb39d11: closer to original llvm code (auto-sync enabled modules)
sx-5dc693efa2: cs_insn.illegal flag added; if set, instruction is decoded correctly but considered illegal
sx-a91708649a: exactfpimm operand did not set the fp field
exactfpimm operand now also sets the fp field
sx-941c786eba: cs_mode_mips_ptr64 is now required to decode 64-bit pointers
sx-bd3130212d: capstone previously did not print instruction aliases by default (or had a different default)
capstone now prints instruction aliases by default
sx-615d9223d7: gnu-style mnemonics were used for legacy packet instructions.
sx-0f442ac77a: immediate and offset operands were interpreted with a different signedness.
the signedness interpretation of immediate and offset operands has been corrected.
sx-f2bfabe4d2: cs_insn had no alias_id field; alias instructions were not distinguished from real instructions
cs_insn now has alias_id field; cs_insn->is_alias flag is set for aliases; cs_insn->alias_id holds the alias id
sx-bd1756d9b1: cs_option with cs_opt_detail used cs_opt_on/cs_opt_off only; alias instructions always returned alias operands
cs_option(handle, cs_opt_detail, cs_opt_detail_real | cs_opt_on) now available to force real instruction operands for aliases; default behavior returns alias operands
sx-25834fcc60: cstool had no -r flag; alias instructions always showed alias operands
cstool now supports -r flag to show real instruction operands for aliases; without -r shows alias operands
sx-285b64b6f7: csh and cs_option's value argument was not uintptr_t
csh and cs_option's value argument is now uintptr_t
sx-9b4bcd6a12: cstool used separate mechanism for architecture-specific options
cstool now uses cstool <arch>+<option> syntax to enable architecture-specific options
sx-5796f521ba: disponent stored as separate reg/imm operand
sx-79cfbc3da7: mem.disp is always positive, subtracted flag indicates subtraction
sx-84d441a2ee: system registers separated with more detail
sx-8c2141e80b: system operands used old capstone encoding
system operands now use llvm's sysm value encoding
sx-a04023b28a: alias instructions present in instruction enum
sx-2edc14f30b: register aliases (r15=pc etc.) printed by default
not printed by default; opt-in via cs_opt_syntax_cs_reg_alias
sx-fd961c539d: arm_op.imm was a smaller integer type
sx-0de00ac08b: post-index memory access had a separate reg/imm operand
post-index memory access has the displacement set inside the memory operand
sx-60696e563f: system registers were stored in cs_arm->reg
system registers are separated out with more detail
sx-286e04b65a: cpu features like mclass, v8 were checked loosely
cpu features like mclass, v8 are checked more strictly
sx-f2330a38a9: writeback was moved into the detail struct
sx-9cd3813ea4: register aliases were printed by default
register aliases are not printed by default; enable with cs_opt_syntax_cs_reg_alias
sx-74f7efe724: completely rewritten branch conditions saving all detail known about the bits
sx-1fdb1f3aab: instruction aliases like bf in the instruction enum
sx-3c0b098f43: register 0 used literally for (ra|0) zero case
ppc_reg_zero register (name '0') used for (ra|0) zero case
sx-c0e187758b: memory operands stored base register in op->reg and absolute address in op->imm
memory operands store base register in m68k_op_mem.base_reg and absolute address in m68k_op_mem.address
sx-0c58c27963: compatibility layer for type identifiers and detail names
sx-a3af2987c7: systemz_cc_o = 0 and systemz_cc_invalid != 0 (values swapped to match llvm)
sx-c56c6799d6: arm64_* (e.g. cs_arch_arm64, arm64_ins_*, arm64_reg_*, arm64_op_*, arm64_ext_*, arm64_sft_*, arm64_cc_*) and arm64_* types (arm64_reg, arm64_cc, cs_arm64, arm64_extender, arm64_shifter, arm64_vas) and detail->arm64
sx-b2209f9d7d: sysz_* (e.g. cs_arch_sysz, sysz_ins_*, sysz_reg_*, sysz_op_*, sysz_cc_*) and sysz_* types (sysz_reg, sysz_cc, cs_sysz, sysz_op_type, sysz_op_mem, sysz_op) and detail->sysz
sx-6af66eca12: cstool required modifying c code and recompiling to enable syntax features
cstool now accepts +suffix options appended to the arch argument (e.g., x32+att, ppc32+percentage)
sx-734101c196: auth command did not exist or had different behavior
auth command now enables certificate-based authentication to obtain kerberos tickets, nt hashes, or establish ldap connections
sx-10159c55f9: in version 4.x, -export was used with -out to specify output filename for pfx/p12
in version 5, -export is a boolean flag (default format) and -out is a separate option for output filename
sx-6e7022340c: in version 4.x, -output was used to specify a filename for output
in version 5, -output is a filename prefix, not a full filename
sx-23ee180b6b: in version 4.x, -text was the default output format
in version 5, -text is an explicit flag to output as formatted text file
sx-91b8870dfd: datetime parsed without exposing kind value
datetime parsed into an object that exposes kind value
sx-68efb6aebe: structure attributes no longer exist by default
sx-95f4e620a8: objects' attributes may not have existed
objects' attributes always exist, but may be none
sx-1dcc72b20d: process.enumeratemodulessync() or the new async equivalent
sx-3ba79086ae: get all exports of a module
sx-81b3fd7855: python 2 compatibility code in wmi and ese modules
sx-d10005c5e3: how long is a ticket valid
sx-d9e5fa0173: anonymous logon was enabled in the smb server
anonymous logon is disabled in the smb server
sx-74a2fb0866: path traversal was possible in smbserver.py (cve-2021-31800)
path checks are hardened and tid checks added
sx-864997136c: anonymous smb 2.x connections are not encrypted anymore
sx-1af676870e: dpapi_system lsa secret was not parsed or shown
dpapi_system lsa secret is now parsed and key contents are shown
sx-24ec145508: smb3 packets were not encrypted by default
smb3 packets are encrypted by default if target server supports smb >= 3
sx-ec8db09246: raw_dn was returned as a pyasn1 object
sx-fb467ce631: strip parameter defaulted to true (or different value)
sx-b5f9a12c8b: modify() did not mutate the passed object
modify() now mutates the passed object
sx-09f926a826: servers in a pool had independent state per connection
servers state is shared between connections using the same pool when single=true
sx-8f424698b1: connections in context manager would bind even with auto_bind_none
connections in context manager no longer bind when auto_bind is set to auto_bind_none
sx-7a3fcc59c7: accessing a missing entry's attribute raised an exception when searching
accessing a missing entry's attribute returns false when searching
sx-c4ac448dc5: formatters raised exceptions when unable to format
formatters return the raw_value when unable to format
sx-95d8191151: to_unicode() has from_server=false parameter, does not try client encoding when decoding server data
sx-597a0870b4: check if schema property is empty
sx-927248d3f9: integer validator automatically converts valid string numbers to int
sx-d23ced7ffc: direct access to config parameters like classes_excluded_from_check, attributes_excluded_from_check, utf8_encoded_syntaxes, utf8_encoded_types
sx-72eb7c39f7: dn sanitization was performed during bind
sx-1311abd855: all_attributes sent '*' in the attribute list while searching
all_attributes does not send any attribute in the attribute list while searching
sx-892bc910a8: connection.entries defaulted to none or similar
sx-23235ac49e: why is my connection trying ipv6
sx-8320aade56: allowed falsy values or none as default
does not permit falsy values or none as default
sx-e3ca0d8c39: response included in exception when raise_exceptions=true
sx-4f2e593fe2: operation result is only in result attribute
sx-1899924ca4: exceptions inherit from ldapexception (subclass of exception)
sx-c80050dccd: auth_anonymous, auth_simple, auth_sasl, search_scope_base_object, search_scope_single_level, search_scope_whole_subtree, search_never_dereference_aliases, search_dereference_in_searching, search_dereference_finding_base_object, search_dereference_always, strategy_sync, strategy_async_threaded, strategy_ldif_producer, strategy_sync_restartable, strategy_reusable_threaded, strategy_mock_sync, strategy_mock_async, pooling_strategy_first, pooling_strategy_round_robin, pooling_strategy_random, get_no_info, get_dsa_info, get_schema_info, get_all_info

anonymous, simple, sasl, base, level, subtree, deref_never, deref_search, deref_base, deref_always, sync, async, ldif, restartable, reusable, mock_sync, mock_async, first, round_robin, random, none, dsa, schema, all
sx-2d5ee477be: reworked to enable throttling of authentications
sx-0fbed11d4b: obfuscation and amsi bypasses were default
sx-f34d5dc051: old semantics of file download and upload flags
new semantics of file download and upload flags
sx-375b3a7656: sam/lsa dump via mssql required local admin
sam/lsa dump via mssql no longer requires local admin
sx-e17122ccb5: smb protocol always performed admin check with sc_manager_all_access
smb protocol now has --no-admin-check flag to skip the admin check
sx-910dab3e45: winrm database stores fqdn instead of netbios name
sx-874d61a417: default secretsdump technique (impacket-based, writes to disk)
regsecretsdump technique (registry queries, no disk writes)
sx-0d712ae02b: restructured laps login to fix login issues
sx-0757f1799f: write without delete was not displayed as write access
write without delete is now displayed as write access
write no delete shown as write
sx-7e803afbfc: winrm required smb to gather ntlm info
winrm no longer needs smb to gather ntlm info
sx-590a2a10ca: jitter option that randomly inserted delays
sx-701df5ddd4: obfuscation enabled by default for powershell
sx-b2880efaa0: amsi bypass turned off by default
sx-dee592d110: default now lists current directory (.) instead of previous behavior
sx-d950d7025e: paramiko automatically discovers private keys in ~/.ssh/
paramiko auto-discovery of private keys in ~/.ssh/ is disabled
sx-514a4c0910: implicit sequential execution of multiple requests
explicit `flow:` field using javascript (ecmascript 5.1 via goja) to orchestrate/conditionally execute requests
sx-1a1a80d627: autofallback to ztls enabled by default
sx-33021b10e3: redirects defined per template, not per request
sx-34bd6a5c32: exclude-ports field overwrites the default reserved ports list entirely
sx-620071c75c: port field only accepted a single port value
port field accepts comma-separated multi-port values
sx-6dd3ecb466: calling a protocol like `ssl()` or `http()` in javascript executed the protocol.
calling a protocol like `ssl()` or `http()` in javascript now requires passing index or id arguments to execute specific requests.
sx-fecda2ba3f: filters applied on installed path of templates by default
filters are applied on installed path of templates and can be customized with manual template path input
sx-b0cc15acea: users could modify .nuclei-ignore file to customize ignore list
.nuclei-ignore file is internal and must not be modified by users; use nuclei configuration file instead
sx-b1e164bb2b: api key was entered via interactive prompt
api key must be set via pdcp_api_key environment variable or credentials file
sx-5cae69d884: using `name` as the dropdown value and `id` as the id value of the dropdown
`name` is the dropdown value, `id` is the id value of the dropdown, `freeform` is for text entry
sx-03646d262b: using `account-id` and `token` for jira cloud, or `account-id` as username and `token` as password for on-prem
sx-81e3a9a804: using `closed_status` variable or a field named `closed-status`
using `status-not` variable in the jira template
sx-3944167ac0: using `severity-as-label` as the only way to send severity
`severity-as-label` is optional; user custom fields for jira cloud are recommended instead
sx-fe8069f891: running all templates by default includes fuzzing/dos templates
fuzzing/dos templates are excluded from default scan and require -itags flag
sx-f807a24c5b: before v3.1.4, multi-protocol templates with `flow` only returned one result
from v3.1.4, nuclei prints all events/results in a template
sx-a005f01089: python 2.7 compatibility code and ability to run on python 2.7
sx-148895acaa: old sqlite storage reader/writer class names
renamed sqlite storage reader/writer class names
sx-35f5cdb6ef: winlnk no longer generates duplicate dlt events
sx-1d2a0a2e3f: data files moved into python module
sx-e11fc6bbb2: sessionstart, sessionconfiguration, sessioncompletion attribute container classes
sx-0361d79d5a: log2timeline.py, psort.py, pinfo.py, psteal.py, image_export.py as standalone scripts
tools migrated to python entry points / console_scripts
sx-de0f0ae81e: sign parameter accepted as positional argument
sx-30c24edb96: process.recv did not raise on stdout close on windows
process.recv raises eoferror when stdout closes on windows
sx-4d2aa98f17: direct commandline scripts invocation (e.g. `pwn asm` as a script)
use the pwn command with subcommands
sx-48acdf7e2e: context.newline default value when context.os is set to "windows"
context.newline changes when setting context.os to "windows"
sx-a5e7315906: tube.readline() returns buffered data on first eof
sx-92204c0ef2: process() replaces the entire environment when env= is passed
process() now allows adding to the existing environment instead of replacing it
sx-fafbc2b297: gdb.debug did not respect the exe parameter and required a non-empty argv
gdb.debug now respects the exe parameter and allows empty argv
sx-5127b19846: gdb.debug went through a shell to launch the debugged process
gdb.debug no longer goes through a shell
sx-2fca3cdb27: asm() no longer emits p2align 2 headers
sx-ebe590e69b: buffer inherited from some other class
sx-29b8a9699f: gdb not terminated by default when using run_in_new_terminal
gdb is terminated by default when using run_in_new_terminal
sx-8076495bb2: process() only looked at system path for the executable
process() also looks at env['path'] to find the executable
sx-b247402545: docker images used python2 by default
docker images use python3 by default
sx-d10dda8e61: tubes.ssh.process() required python 2 installed on remote
tubes.ssh.process() no longer requires python 2 on remote (still requires python)
sx-337d243bb1: flat(..., filler=) with str values or python2 bytes may have worked differently
flat(..., filler=) is fixed for str values and python2 bytes
sx-8fcf6fb9ad: pwntools requires six v1.12.0 or higher
sx-2581040dcf: flat() and fit() were separate functions
flat() and fit() are now the same function
sx-32ed09821f: old undocumented mechanism for changing logging colors
sx-bc57587480: packing.fit() treated large offsets as raw integers
packing.fit() now treats large offsets as cyclic patterns
sx-efe5deb12d: sh_string returned empty string for empty input
sx-d70f84dc76: rop class used hard-coded value of 4 for bytes
sx-de3e1bb972: ssh.process() returned a generic process object
sx-e753b488ef: sh_string emits strings that are no longer copy-paste compatible
sx-53215a7725: shellcraft.sh did not provide argv[0] or set argc==1
shellcraft.sh now provides argv[0] and sets argc==1 on all platforms
sx-971864f978: stdout and stderr are now ptys by default
sx-26d484e41f: respondto/dontrespondto had a bug causing incorrect behaviour
the respondto/dontrespondto filtering now works correctly
sx-dc09c8fe00: smtp credential interception had a soft failure causing missed captures
smtp credential interception now correctly captures credentials that were previously missed
sx-17a82d92d4: http basic auth parsing broke when password contained colons
http basic auth parsing now correctly handles passwords containing colons
sx-677c4f2b36: proxy-auth had a potential disruption issue
sx-6b10a6be30: static default certificates bundled with responder
sx-582c5fc97f: static certificates bundled with the tool
sx-eaadb649e9: config dump in a new separate log file
sx-994e36d9d3: responder is now less verbose by default; use -v for verbose mode
sx-c5f1baf0a2: several cli options were available directly on the command line
those cli options have been removed and replaced by a configuration file (responder.conf)
sx-c55f2c42f8: could bind to a specific interface on osx
sx-13860c5169: responder included additional resource records in srv answers
responder no longer includes additional rrs in srv answers
sx-b1cd4d09c2: pc was not guaranteed to be synced/consistent in all cases
pc is now guaranteed to be consistent in all cases via an architecture-agnostic mechanism
sx-63e5cdaa50: memory read/write operations had a size limit
sx-e017295664: prints a warning instead of returning uc_err_arg
sx-cccda08442: unicorn allocated 2gb memory for every instance
unicorn no longer allocates 2gb memory upfront; memory is committed only once used, upper limit adjustable via uc_ctl
sx-b87ebcdd91: vex.l could be set without explicit failure
unicorn now fails explicitly when vex.l is set
sx-ca084318ac: uc_mode_armbe8 and uc_mode_arm926 had colliding values
sx-e1cc6a28e3: unicorn v1 provided a 1:1 mapping of virtual address to physical address by default
unicorn 2 no longer provides 1:1 virtual-to-physical mapping by default; must enable via uc_ctl_tlb_mode(uc_tlb_virtual)
sx-fccba0e1f8: uc_emu_start did not return an error if an error within memory hooks was not handled
uc_emu_start now returns an error if any error within memory hooks is not handled
sx-ebe557a6bc: unicorn allowed stopping within mips delay slots and arm it blocks, losing branch information
unicorn 2 makes execution unstoppable within mips delay slots and arm it blocks to preserve correct semantics
sx-5076ce05ef: volatility 2 proxy objects that emulated host methods but were a different type (could cause ordering-dependent failures like x + y vs y + x)
sx-52ee34d6d8: volatility 2 re-read data from the underlying layer each time (allowing live memory changes to be reflected during plugin execution)
sx-66f1ae2d01: volatility 2 stored metadata (layer, offset) as attributes on the object itself, which could conflict with arbitrary member names
sx-d358c4ca8c: in volatility 2, size could only be determined from a constructed object (requiring instantiation on an empty buffer)
sx-b79f8e34e9: address spaces (strictly stacked, one atop another)
sx-9f3938bcc1: automagic was automatic and not user-configurable
automagic processes are codified, can be enabled/disabled, and are selected per-plugin (linux vs windows)
sx-3bfa955c88: plugins could produce output in various formats
all plugins must produce output in a treegrid object
sx-f1a1658d5e: options inherited automatically from base class (e.g. pid)
options must be explicitly declared in requirements
sx-262857505c: memory layer declared in get_requirements() and accessed through context.layers
sx-17ae170019: plugin options defined by class inheritance/argparse config
plugin options declared in get_requirements() as requirements.* objects
sx-996c58b291: `{` must be escaped as `\{` in regex
sx-6325168c83: invalid escape sequences silently fall back to literal character
sx-afdbc30c8f: no minimum length for base64 patterns
sx-14eced8839: same alphabet required for both `base64` and `base64wide` in one string
different alphabets allowed for `base64` and `base64wide` in same string
sx-f86000eca0: wildcards in `of` statement rule names are invalid
sx-28c5d9292c: bytes before/after xored pattern are not xored before alphanumeric check
bytes before/after xored pattern are xored before alphanumeric check
sx-27f0df6944: jump bounds only accept base-10 values
jump bounds accept hex and octal values
sx-5e82f4f139: each `global` or `private` modifier can appear only once
